texts.full_text: S. 190 and Thibodaux Road in Tangipahoa Parish under a written lease[1] executed by Ellis and Ruth Brown Thibodauxin which is contained a stipulation that lessors bound "themselves, their heirs and assigns * * * not to sell or lease all or any part of the adjoining" property owned by them to anyone who would engage in a competitive business during the period of the lease and for a period of one year thereafter—sued Floyd Lavigne and Mrs. Margaret Lavigne, who had purchased the adjoining property from the Thibodauxs, for damages and an injunction to prevent the Lavignes from operating a service station on the property. The plaintiff alleged that the sale of the property to the Lavignes, without the benefit of the restrictive covenant, constituted a breach of the lease agreement. The trial court rendered judgment in favor of the plaintiff, enjoining the Lavignes from operating a service station on the property and awarding damages. The defendants appealed, and the Court of Appeal affirmed the trial court's judgment. The defendants then applied to this Court for writs, which were granted. The principal issue before this Court is whether the restrictive covenant in the lease, which was intended to protect the plaintiff's business from competition, is binding on the Lavignes, who were not parties to the original lease and who purchased the property without actual knowledge of the covenant. After a thorough review of the lease agreement and the applicable law, we find that the restrictive covenant in the lease is a personal covenant that does not run with the land. The covenant was intended to benefit the plaintiff during the term of the lease and for

This forum meeting will explore the nexus of environmental risk, humanitarian crisis and climate change, and identify ways to better prepare for and respond to environmental emergencies. The Forum will bring together representatives from governments, industry, academia, aid agencies and civil society share the common goal of helping countries effectively respond to environmental emergencies. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Norway and the Norwegian Directorate for Civil Protection are hosting the 2015 Forum. We do not sell or rent your email address to other companies. When you sign up for email communications from WineExpress.com, you may be contacted by our partner companies, Wine Enthusiast Catalog and Wine Enthusiast Magazine, about their products, services, and promotions that may be of interest to you. Each of our affiliates has a privacy policy that is consistent with our own... All information that is collected is on an ‘opt-in’ basis and users are given the opportunity to ‘opt-out’ at any time. Why do you collect tax? Because we are located in New York, state law requires that WineExpress.com collect sales tax for all orders. Rather than adding it into prices or combining it with shipping rates, like some other web sites, we list tax separately so that you can clearly understand all charges. Technical/Web Site questions: Do you offer discounts on higher quantity orders? Yes, all wines have an “up to 15%” case discount. You can order a mixed case and each wine will be discounted at its individual discount rate. Case discounts are not applicable to samplers, wine clubs, gift baskets or sale wines. How can I find newly added wines? We have an RSS feed which updates as new wines are added to the site, you can find them here. Why do Champagne orders take longer than other orders? Champagne orders require additional due diligence. Therefore, all Champagne orders require 3-5 days processing. Unfortunately, express delivery is not available on Champagne. How do I know the size of the wine bottle? All wines are 750 mL, standard bottle size, unless indicated otherwise in the product description or title. We hold that Womack did not receive ineffective assistance of counsel, and we affirm the district court’s denial of his petition for habeas cor- pus. FACTS AND PROCEDURAL HISTORY Womack shared an apartment with Kathryn Reeder, her seven and thirteen year-old sons, and her twelve year-old daughter. On October 4, 1999, while Reeder was at work, Womack stabbed the thirteen year-old boy in the neck, chest and shoulder, cut the seven year-old boy across his neck and chest, and locked all three children in the bathroom. After stealing several items, Womack fled the apartment. Reeder’s daughter escaped from the bathroom, ran to Reeder’s work- place, and informed her mother what Womack had done. Reeder and her daughter returned to her apartment and Reeder called 911 when she saw the extent of her sons’ injuries. Womack was arrested the following day when he attempted to cash a forged check. A grand jury indicted Womack on two counts of attempted murder with use of a deadly weapon, three counts of first degree kidnapping of a minor with use of a deadly weapon with substantial bodily harm, three counts of robbery with use of a deadly weapon, and one count each of burglary, forgery, and possession of credit or debit card without cardholder con- sent. texts.full_text: nosis by fine needle aspiration cytology. To describe the fine needle aspiration cytology findings of polymorphous low grade adenocarcinoma of the salivary gland. The authors reviewed the cytologic findings of 5 cases of polymorphous low grade adenocarcinoma. All cases were confirmed by histologic examination. All cases showed similar cytologic findings. The smears had high cellularity with a population of mixed epithelial and myoepithelial cells. These cells formed branching papillae, sheets and clusters. The epithelial cells were uniform, with round to ovoid nuclei; absent or inconspicuous nucleoli; and a moderate amount of dense cytoplasm. Occasionally the cells formed spherical structures containing hyaline globules. Myxoid matrix, either dispersed in the background or associated with cell groups was also observed. The myoepithelial component showed large polygonal cells with abundant eosinophilic to clear cytoplasm and vesicular nuclei. No significant cytological atypia or mitoses were identified. The sustainability of these effects and the long-term results have been under question. To compare bariatric surgery versus medical management (MM) for T2D based on a meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (RCTs) with 2 years of follow-up. Seven RHere's a summary of the seven RCTs included in the meta-analysis comparing bariatric surgery versus medical management for T2D with a 2-year follow-up: 1. RCT by Schauer et al. (2012) - DiREкт Trial - Comparison: Roux-en-Y gastric bypass (RYGB) vs. MM - Duration: 2 years - Results: RYGB led to a significantly greater reduction in HbA1c (-2.0% vs. -0.9%) and a higher rate of T2D remission (95% vs. 12%) compared to MM. 2. History of the museum These baths were originally located outside the city walls of ancient Side, built in the middle of the 4th century AD. The baths had been constructed earlier, in the 2nd century AD, and then rebuilt in the 5th century AD. Their plan exemplifies so-called ring-baths. In contrast to the previously built Roman baths, where customers walked through the rooms arranged in a row one after the other, according to a new concept, they wandered around the baths in a circle. The building was erected on a rectangular plan, with the sides of 26 and 41.5 meters in length respectively. Following completion of assessments, the Individualized EducationPlan (IEP) Team, of which parents are essential members, meets to review and discuss findings in order to make a determination of eligibility under one of the established disability categories.Whathappens next?Once a child is determined to have a disability,the IEP Team works together to develop the IEP, a written document identifying the special education and related services necessary to support the child'sdevelopment andeducational program. The IEP allows everyone with a role in teaching and nurturing the child to understand what the child will need to grow and prosper, and the particular role they can play as a supportive member of the child's team. Services identified on an IEP might include special instruction, speech and language therapy, physical and occupational therapy, assistive technology devices and services, psychological services, and other services identified by the IEP Team as necessary for the child to benefit from participating in an educational program. Most tumors were supraglottic. 5 out of 6 tumors were clinically benign, without any metastases or recurrence after exclusive surgical resectionand radiotherapy when required. The incidence rate we found for laryngeal and hypophayrnx BSC was approximately $2\%$ in our series. This is lower than what has been described elsewhere, where rates ranging from 4 to over a thousand per ten million individuals have been reported based on regional differences or selection biases. All patients presented with hoarseness as the initial symptom and all cases were stage I-II according TNM classification for cancer of head neck (UICC/AJCC). Tumor size ranged between $1 \text{ cm}$ to more than three centimeters. All tumors showed well-defined margins in postoperative pathology reports, which is consistent with the aggressive local behavior typically seen. Histologically BSC presents as solid nests of unifOrm basaloid cells intermixed within sheets or cords (keratinizing and non- keratized squamous). The tumor was entirely composed by small round neoplastic cells without significant atypia, mitotic activity. However there are some foci in which were observed individual cell necrosis. The immunohistochemical study for the six cases showed that all tumors stained positive with CK 7 and EMA antibodies (100%). Three of them stains negative while other three only partially expressed Ber- EP4 antigen, neuron-specific enolase was detected to be fully阳性 in five out-of-six lesions. Insert data into this new page split test that will cause pages to be filled up and require splitting, but don't allow them too (by filling all of it). 3. The result is an incorrect "page number" in some places. To reproduce the issue: 1) Create a table with innodb_file_per_table enabled. 2) Insert data into this new page split test that will cause pages to be filled up and require splitting, but don't allow them too (by filling all of it). 3) The result is an incorrect "page number" in some places. The issue seems related the way InnoDB splits a root node when there are multiple rows on each leaf. If we split using one algorithm for lower page numbers then another upper pages, and if these algorithms don't line up correctly with their respective boundaries (which they do not), it can cause an off-by-one error in some places. This is only observed to happen under certain conditions of data distribution - specifically when there are many rows on a single leaf. It seems that the "symmetric" split mode, which tries for equal-sized splits above and below middle

texts.full_text: explicit experience of social rejection on cardiac signs of "freezing". The study aimed at investigating the immediate cardiac effect of the sudden perception of other people's laughter after experimentally manipulating healthy participants' proneness to experience laughter as a cue of social threat. We expected that participants would show cardiac signs of freezing (i.e., sustained heart rate deceleration immediately after perception of the laughter) after prior social rejection but not or less so after prior acceptance, due to an increased bias to perceive the ambiguous social signal a-laughter-as-putative-social-threat when having recently experienced social exclusion. Fifty-two healthy participants (25 males; age 19–30 years), who were all naïve with respect of their hypothesis and had not been previously tested in this laboratory, performed two randomized within-subjects experimental sessions. Each session comprised an induction procedure to putatively prime the participants' affective response system either towards a state characterized by threat perception (social exclusion) or security states prone more easily towards trust behaviors (being included). Subsequently after having undergone social rejection vs inclusion manipulations through audio-visual clips, each participant watched three sets of five 20-s video-clips displaying in turn happy laughing children and adults. At the end they filled a self-report questionnaire to assess their perceived experience during viewing. Afterward participants were found that cardiac freezing reactions occurred only after prior social exclusion but not inclusion (being excluded) manipulations, i.e., P(Heart Rate Deceleration Before vs After Laughter)>0 . 95). Raul Labrador, one of the most conservative members of the House Republican conference, has helped engineer a bill that would overhaul decades-old federal sentencing guidelines, giving judges more leeway and offering criminals the hope of less time in prison. It would seem an unusual move for Labrador, an Idaho Republican whose membership in the Freedom Caucus has put him at the center of efforts to oust Speaker John Boehner and shake up the House of Representatives’ leadership. But Labrador says criminal justice issues have been a top priority since his election to the House in 2010. After a career as an attorney at a firm that specialized in immigration and criminal defense, Labrador said, he saw the consequences of judges being stripped of flexibility and forced to comply with mandatory minimum sentences for drug crimes...The bill is now moving through Congress after clearing its first major hurdle — approval by House Judiciary Committee. texts.full_text: the question many of us ask. Why did our immigrant ancestor chose to live in Cleveland over Pittsburgh? Little Rock over Los Angeles? Memphis over Miami? The answers might lie in the records of the Industrial Removal Office, a scary name for a good organization. The IRO, founded in 1901, assisted immigrants in finding employment and better living conditions, and helped assimilate them into American society. IRO agents, often working in partnership with B’nai B’rith or other Jewish fraternal groups, spread around the USA securing jobs, and then immigrants would be sent to those locations towork specific trades: hat makers went from New York City/New Jersey; furriers moved west (and south) across Pennsylvania into Ohio. The records of IRO can help you find your immigrant ancestor’s port-of entry as well an understanding why they settled where and when the did in America, so please join me on Tuesday August 2nd at 1:05 PM for a look back to move forward! The Eastern European Mutt is going…to Ukraine. On my second trip since January of this year I’ll be leading another tour with JewishGen SIGs and FGS Tours from July the seventh through twenty-second, flying in on United Airlines Flight 1058 which leaves Chicago at ten minutes before midnight Central Time for an arrival into Kiev Boryspil International Airport a few hours later. It will take us about two weeks to make our way around Ukraine visiting sites of interest and researching as we go along! I’ll be speaking…to the New York Genealogical & Biographical Society on September

texts.full_text: the efficacy of oral theophylline compared with placebo in people with stable chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). Systematic review of trials comparing oral theophylline with placebo for a minimum of 7 days in people with stable COPD. Twenty randomized-controlled trials were included in this review. The following outcomes showed improvement with theophylline compared with placebo: FEV1 and FVC both improved with theophylline (weighted mean difference [WMD] 0.10 L; 95% confidence interval [95% CI] 0.04-0.16 and WMD 0.21 L; 95% CI 0.10-0.32, respectively). VO2 max also improved with theophylline (WMD 0.63 mL/kg/min; 95% CI 0.23-1.03). However, there was no significant difference in the rate of exacerbations (WMD -0.04; 95% CI -0.13 to 0.05) or in the quality of life scores (WMD -0.05; 95% CI -0.15 to 0.05) between the theophylline and placebo groups. texts.full_text: general, various processes may be required to manufacture semiconductor devices. The processes may be classified into thin film deposition, photolithography, etching, and/or ion implantation. Thin film deposition may be for depositing a thin film on a wafer (a semiconductor substrate), for example, a silicon wafer; photolithography and/or etching may be for forming the deposited thin film in a pattern that may or may not be predetermined; and/or ion implantation may be for implanting impurities into the wafer. In particular, photolithography may be a process for forming a photoresist film on a thin film deposited on a wafer, and/or exposing and/or developing the photoresist film using an exposure unit and/or a reticle (mask) installed in the exposure unit, thereby forming a photoresist pattern. Methodology and Detection Principle: The study employs the Askaryan effect, where high-energy particles generate a charged particle excess in the atmosphere, leading to Cherenkov radiation. This radiation is detected as radio waves by the Yakutsk array, which consists of large antennas suitable for low-frequency (32 MHz) detection. 2. Lateral Distribution Function (LDF): The paper introduces a generalized formula for the LDF, which depends on the primary particle's energy (E0) and the depth of maximum particle density (X_max). The formula shows good agreement with data at average and large distances from the shower axis, indicating its validity in these regions. 3. X_max Determination: By analyzing the ratio of radio emission at distances 175m and 725m, the authors determined X_max for a specific energy of 3.7×10^19 eV. texts.full_text: his own experience. Buddhist teachings consists of philosophy (wisdom) , ethics (kindness and compassion), andmeditation practices. Dharma, the core teaching in Buddhism derived from Buddha's direct experiences with reality during meditation practice is often translated as "the truth" or “right conduct.” It encompasses a wide array of teachings that guide individuals towards enlightenment and nirvana—a state where one transcends suffering (dukkha) by understanding the true nature of existence. Buddhists believe in following Dharma, which is not just about adhering to rules or rituals but also involves living according to ethical principles such as compassion for others. These teachings are based on Buddha's insights into human psychology and behavior that he gained through his deep meditation practice over many years under the Bodhi tree. The philosophy of Buddhism emphasizes understanding impermanence (anicca), suffering, craving (tanha) , attachment or clinging to existence in its current form as illusory. By recognizing these truths about life's nature and practicing mindfulness along with ethical conducts such as compassion for all beings without discrimination is considered the path of Dharma. The ethics component involves cultivating virtues like generosity, patience (kshama), truth-telling ("satya"), refraining from harming others or lying in thought word action. These are not just moral guidelines but also ways to cultivate wisdom and compassion within oneself leading towards enlightenment for the benefit of all sentient beings. The meditation practices that form part of Dharma include mindfulness (sati), concentration, insight into impermanence ("anicca"), suffering "dukkha", non-self or anatta) , craving tanha). These meditative techniques help practitioners to understand and break free from the cycle of birth-death-suffering known as samsara. The teachings are not static; they evolve with time, adapting themselves according to different cultures. The essence remains constant—the path towards enlightenment is through understanding Dharma: wisdom (Panna), ethical conduct ("Sila"), and meditation practice "Samadhi".
